{"score": 8, "seo_title": "trees drink their own rain before it hits the ground", "yt_tags": ["trees", "canopy", "rainfall", "nature", "science"], "topic_hasgetag": "Forests", "thumbnail_text": "TREES SIP RAIN", "hook": "They sip before it even hits", "script": "They sip before it even hits. {"score": 8, "seo_title": "Mycelium: the forest's hidden network", "yt_tags": ["fungi", "mycelium", "underground threads", "nature", "earth science"], "topic_hashtag": "Fungi", "thumbnail_text": "FUNGAL INTERNET", "hook": "Watch this log breathe", "script": "Watch this log breathe. So who’s getting the memo right now?", "sentiment": "positive"}

{"score": 7, "seo_title": "Streams carve the land in plain sight", "yt_tags": ["streams", "erosion", "rocky landscapes", "nature", "earth science"], "topic_hashtag": "Freshwater", "thumbnail_text": "STREAM ENGINE", "hook": "Watch the land move here", "script": "Watch the land move here. That quiet stream isn’t just flowing—it’s slicing through rock like a knife through warm butter. Peek at the swirl where the water hits the stone, that’s where the real work happens. The current carries tiny grains that act like sandpaper, scraping and deepening the channel over time. That’s why valleys get carved without a single earthquake. So, what shape do you think this stream will carve in a thousand years?", "sentiment": "positive"}

{"score": 9, "seo_title": "Rock layers record Earth's slow-motion collapse", "yt_tags": ["rock layers", "erosion", "alpine landscapes", "geology", "science"], "topic_hashtag": "Geology", "thumbnail_text": "ROCKS TELL TALES", "hook": "These cliffs are falling apart", "script": "These cliffs are falling apart because ice and rain carve them like butter.
